perturbation.original_text: 1.2 “Confidential Information ” means any information or other data relating to one p arty (the “Disclosing Party”) and/or the business carried on o r proposed or intended to be carried on by that party and which is made available for the purpos es of the Proposal to the other party (the “Receiving Party”) or its Agents by the Disclosing Par ty or its Agents or recorded in agreed minutes following oral disclosure and any other info rmation otherwise made available by the Disclosing Party or its Agents to the Receiving Party or its Agents, whether before, on or after the date of this Agreement, and whether in writing or otherw ise, including any information, analysis or specifications derived from, containing or reflecting such information but excluding information which:- a) is publicly available at the time of its disclosure or becomes publicly available (other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party or any of its Agents contrary to the terms of this Agreement); or b) was lawfully in the possession of the Receiving Party or its Agents (as can be demonstrated by its written records or other reasonable evidence) fr ee of any restriction as to its use or disclosure prior to its being so disclosed; or
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 “Confidential Information ” means any information or other data relating to one p arty (the “Disclosing Party”) and/or the business carried on o r proposed or intended to be carried on by that party and which is made available for the purpos es of the Proposal to the other party (the “Receiving Party”) or its Agents by the Disclosing Par ty or its Agents or recorded in agreed minutes following oral disclosure and any other info rmation otherwise made available by the Disclosing Party or its Agents to the Receiving Party or its Agents, whether before, on or after the date of this Agreement, and whether in writing or otherw ise, including any information, analysis or specifications derived from, containing or reflecting such information. This definition includes all information, irrespective of whether it is already publicly known. Therefore, no exclusions apply. a) is publicly available at the time of its disclosure or becomes publicly available (other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party or any of its Agents contrary to the terms of this Agreement); or b) was lawfully in the possession of the Receiving Party or its Agents (as can be demonstrated by its written records or other reasonable evidence) fr ee of any restriction as to its use or disclosure prior to its being so disclosed; or
perturbation.explanation: The modification to the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1.2 creates an in-text contradiction. The original definition specifically excludes publicly available information. The added sentence states that all information is included, 'irrespective of whether it is already publicly known,' thus negating the following exclusions that are defined under 'a) is publicly available at the time of its disclosure or becomes publicly available'. This creates ambiguity and introduces an internal contradiction within the defintion itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 “Confidential Information ” means any information or other data relating to one p arty (the “Disclosing Party”) and/or the business carried on o r proposed or intended to be carried on by that party and which is made available for the purpos es of the Proposal to the other party (the “Receiving Party”) or its Agents by the Disclosing Par ty or its Agents or recorded in agreed minutes following oral disclosure and any other info rmation otherwise made available by the Disclosing Party or its Agents to the Receiving Party or its Agents, whether before, on or after the date of this Agreement, and whether in writing or otherw ise, including any information, analysis or specifications derived from, containing or reflecting such information. This definition includes all information, irrespective of whether it is already publicly known. Therefore, no exclusions apply. a) is publicly available at the time of its disclosure or becomes publicly available (other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party or any of its Agents contrary to the terms of this Agreement); or b) was lawfully in the possession of the Receiving Party or its Agents (as can be demonstrated by its written records or other reasonable evidence) fr ee of any restriction as to its use or disclosure prior to its being so disclosed; or
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the sentence 'This definition includes all information, irrespective of whether it is already publicly known. Therefore, no exclusions apply.' This directly contradicts the subsequent clauses 'a) is publicly available at the time of its disclosure or becomes publicly available' and 'b) was lawfully in the possession of the Receiving Party or its Agents...free of any restriction as to its use or disclosure'. The changed text explicitly states that no exclusions apply, negating the stated exclusions within the same definition. This creates a clear in-text contradiction, as the definition both includes and excludes publicly available information.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 All Confidential Information given by one party to th is Agreement (the “Disclosing Party”) to the other party (the “Receiving Party”) will be treated by the Receiving Party as secret and confidential and will not, without the Disclosing Pa rty’s written consent, directly or indirectly communicate or disclose (whether in writing or orally o r in any other manner) Confidential Information to any other person other than in accord ance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 All Confidential Information given by one party to th is Agreement (the “Disclosing Party”) to the other party (the “Receiving Party”) will be treated by the Receiving Party as secret and confidential and may, without the Disclosing Pa rty’s written consent, directly or indirectly communicate or disclose (whether in writing or orally o r in any other manner) Confidential Information to any other person other than in accord ance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will not' to 'may' in Section 2.1 regarding the disclosure of Confidential Information introduces a direct contradiction. The original clause strictly prohibits disclosure without written consent. The modified clause suggests disclosure is permissible even without consent, leading to ambiguity about the Receiving Party's obligations. The statement now contradicts what 'Confidential Information' means, since now there is a question on how confidential it is, if it can be disclosed without any consequence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 All Confidential Information given by one party to th is Agreement (the “Disclosing Party”) to the other party (the “Receiving Party”) will be treated by the Receiving Party as secret and confidential and may, without the Disclosing Pa rty’s written consent, directly or indirectly communicate or disclose (whether in writing or orally o r in any other manner) Confidential Information to any other person other than in accord ance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'will not' to 'may' in Section 2.1 directly contradicts the original intention of the clause, which was to strictly prohibit the disclosure of confidential information without written consent. The modified clause introduces ambiguity, suggesting that disclosure is permissible even without consent. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as it undermines the confidentiality of the information and conflicts with the purpose of the agreement regarding the treatment of confidential information, making the terms uncertain and potentially unenforceable. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, which shows that the contradiction is present in the same section after the change was introduced. Since, according to the prompt, that doens't necessarily mean there wasn't a contradiction, based on the explanation given, there is indeed a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5 DURATION The obligations of each party and its Agents under thi s Agreement shall survive the termination of any discussions or negotiations between the parties regardi ng the Proposal and continue thereafter for a period of 5 (five) years.
perturbation.changed_text: 5 DURATION The obligations of each party and its Agents under thi s Agreement shall survive the termination of any discussions or negotiations between the parties regardi ng the Proposal and continue thereafter for a period of 5 (five) years, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties at the time of termination.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties at the time of termination' to Section 5 introduces a contradictory element. While the original clause sets a fixed 5-year duration, the addition allows for a potentially shorter or even immediate termination of obligations, contingent on a future agreement. This contradicts the initially defined duration and creates uncertainty, since the duration is not definite. The term for duration is not clear, and how confidential the information could be after this termination agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5 DURATION The obligations of each party and its Agents under thi s Agreement shall survive the termination of any discussions or negotiations between the parties regardi ng the Proposal and continue thereafter for a period of 5 (five) years, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties at the time of termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement continues for a period of 5 years. The changed text adds 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties at the time of termination.' This addition introduces a contradiction because it allows the agreement to be terminated earlier than the initially stated 5 years, making the duration uncertain and creating a direct conflict with the original fixed duration. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, since the change is introducing an element that will affect the clause itself. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 The Receiving Party undertakes to comply with South A frica’s general privacy protection in terms of the Bill of Rights (Section 14) in connection wit h this Proposal and shall procure that its personnel shall observe the provisions of such Act (as applicable) or any amendments and re- enactments thereof and any regulations made pursuant the reto.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 The Receiving Party undertakes to comply with South A frica’s general privacy protection in terms of the Bill of Rights (Section 14) in connection wit h this Proposal and shall endeavor to procure that its personnel shall observe the provisions of such Act (as applicable) or any amendments and re- enactments thereof and any regulations made pursuant the reto.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall procure' to 'shall endeavor to procure' in Section 8.1 introduces a contradiction concerning the level of commitment required regarding privacy protection. 'Shall procure' implies a mandatory obligation to ensure personnel comply with privacy laws. 'Shall endeavor to procure' suggests a weaker, effort-based obligation, potentially conflicting with the initial undertaking to comply with privacy protection. The lack of clear obligation on whether it will definitely happen contradicts with the concept of Data Protection, if that can't be ensured.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 The Receiving Party undertakes to comply with South A frica’s general privacy protection in terms of the Bill of Rights (Section 14) in connection wit h this Proposal and shall endeavor to procure that its personnel shall observe the provisions of such Act (as applicable) or any amendments and re- enactments thereof and any regulations made pursuant the reto.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall procure' to 'shall endeavor to procure' introduces a weaker obligation in Section 8.1. While both the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text are in the same location, the explanation clarifies that the original text established a mandatory obligation for privacy protection, while the changed text creates a weaker, effort-based one. This shift to a weaker obligation creates a contradiction because it undermines the initial 'undertaking to comply' by not guaranteeing personnel compliance, thus creating uncertainty and a potential breach of data protection requirements. This adheres to the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction', where obligations are defined inconsistently, leading to unclear enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: 9.6 This Agreement will be governed by and construed in acco rdance with South African law and the parties irrevocably submit to the exclusive jurisdict ion of the South African courts.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.6 This Agreement will be governed by and construed in acco rdance with South African law and the parties irrevocably submit to the jurisdiction of the chosen jurisdiction by the Transnet.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 9.6 removes the 'exclusive jurisdiction of the South African courts' and replaces it with 'the jurisdiction of the chosen jurisdiction by the Transnet.' This creates a conflict because while the agreement is stated to be governed by South African law, Transnet can choose any jurisdiction to solve legal disputes. The legal implications and requirements may not be the same on both selected locations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.6 This Agreement will be governed by and construed in acco rdance with South African law and the parties irrevocably submit to the jurisdiction of the chosen jurisdiction by the Transnet.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9.6 replaces the 'exclusive jurisdiction of the South African courts' with 'the jurisdiction of the chosen jurisdiction by the Transnet.' This directly contradicts the original intention of the agreement being governed by South African law as disputes can now be resolved in a jurisdiction chosen by Transnet, creating a strong in-text contradiction and potential unenforceability. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, indicating that the modification is the source of the contradiction, in accordance with the definition provided.
